examples: Objective To study effective therapy of radioactive esophagitis.
Objective To discuss the dynamic change of reflux esophagitis in morphology.
Esophagitis can also trigger esophageal bleeding or ulcers after frequent exposure of the esophagus to stomach acid.
Over time, if untreated, acid reflux leads to inflammation (esophagitis) and difficulty in swallowing that can cause a partial closing of the lower esophagus (stricture).
